zh.lifeSummary: Paul Thielen於1515年出生在德國。他至少有1個兒子。他去世在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Paul Thielen于1515年出生在德国。他至少有1个儿子。他去世在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Thielen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German and Dutch: patronymic from   Thiel  .

variants.name: Thelen
meanings: Dutch and North German: patronymic from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal name Thietilo. Thelen is a variant of Thielen .

Swedish (Thelén): ornamental or habitational name from the element Thel- of uncertain meaning (see Thelander ) + the adjectival suffix -én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’. Compare Thelin .

variants.name: Thiel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Egon, Franz, Reinhard, Wolfgang, Angelika, Arno, Bernhard.

North German, Dutch, and Flemish: from a shortened form of the personal name Thietilo, an early medieval pet form of Dietrich (German), Diederik (Dutch), or from some other compound name with the same first element.
Germanized form of Sorbian, Polish, and Czech   Tyl  , a name which is (at least in part) of the same German origin as 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Paul
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Paulus, a Roman family name, originally a nickname meaning ‘small’, used in the post-classical period as a given name. Pre-eminently this is the name of the saint who is generally regarded, with St Peter , as co-founder of the Christian Church. Born in Tarsus, and originally named Saul, he was both a Roman citizen and a Jew, and at first found employment as a minor official persecuting Christians. He was converted to Christianity by a vision of Christ while on the road to Damascus, and thereafter undertook extensive missionary journeys, converting people, especially Gentiles, to Christianity all over the eastern Mediterranean. His preaching aroused considerable official hostility, and eventually he was beheaded at Rome in about ad 65 . He is the author of the fourteen epistles to churches and individuals which form part of the New Testament. It has been in continuous use in the British Isles since the 16th century.

variants.name: Paulus

Born in Tarsus, and originally named Saul, he was both a Roman citizen and a Jew, and at first found employment as a minor official persecuting Christians. He was converted to Christianity by a vision of Christ while on the road to Damascus, and thereafter undertook extensive missionary journeys, converting people, especially Gentiles, to Christianity all over the eastern Mediterranean. His preaching aroused considerable official hostility, and eventually he was beheaded at Rome in about ad 65 . He is the author of the fourteen epistles to churches and individuals which form part of the New Testament. It has been in continuous use in the British Isles since the 16th century.

en.lifeSummary: Paul Thielen was born in 1515, in Germany. He had at least 1 son. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Paul Thielen est né en 1515, à Allemagne. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Paul Thielen wurde 1515 in Deutschland geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Paul Thielen è nato il nel 1515 nel Germania. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Paul Thielenは1515年にドイツで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Paul Thielen(은)는 1515년 독일에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Paul Thielen nasceu em 1515, em Alemanha. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Paul Thielen родился в 1515 г. в местности Германия. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Paul Thielen nació en 1515, en Alemania. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en su pueblo.
